steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a principle: regional sports were given only one-time showcase opportunities at past Olympics, implying international sports had different (better) fates.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: The second sentence provides specific examples of this principle in action. Speed skating (an international sport) was upgraded to permanent medal event status after demonstration, while glíma (a regional sport popular in Sweden) remained a one-time appearance at the 1912 Stockholm Olympics—exactly matching the one-time pattern described for regional sports. Accordingly (meaning “in line with that principle” or “as this pattern shows”) logically connects the general rule to these supporting examples. The other choices fail: Granted introduces a concession rather than exemplification, However suggests contradiction when the examples actually confirm the principle, and Moreover adds unrelated information rather than illustrating what was established. That matches choice C — C is correct.
